steps.title: Identify the goal: explain the 50 Completers challenge to a new audience
steps.content: A new audience needs to understand (1) what a 50 Completer is and (2) what it takes to earn that status. Any choice that omits the definition or leaves the goal undefined fails.

steps.title: Test each choice against the goal
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Hikers aiming to count themselves among the 50 Completers must reach not only West Virginia's Spruce Knob but also the even higher peak of Mount Elbert Colorado.': never defines what a 50 Completer is or that it requires all fifty states — a new audience learns nothing essential. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Not until after you have reached the highest points in all fifty US states—including Spruce Knob in West Virginia and Mount Elbert in Colorado—can you include yourself among the 50 Completers of the Highpointers Club.': defines the challenge (highest points in all fifty states), names the club, and uses the examples to illustrate — gives a new audience everything it needs. ✓
  • Choice C — 'If you are looking for a new hiking challenge, consider joining the Highpointers Club, as did In Suk Han, a hiker who successfully reached the highest point in every US state.': mentions reaching every state's highest point but never defines "50 Completer" or explains the challenge by name. ✗
  • Choice D — 'On November 15, 2013, In Suk Han finally completed the feat of reaching the highest point in all fifty US states, including Spruce Knob in West Virginia and Mount Elbert in Colorado.': describes one person's accomplishment but never explains what a 50 Completer is or presents it as a defined challenge. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
